guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Forest route and spatial proof show one clearer cool/moist/sheltered relationship without more route count.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Protect station/map/request transitions into forest routes after edits; no new chapter shell.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Deepen one forest note or prompt around moisture, shade, nurse wood, or root shelter in a compact relation-first way.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Polish one forest traversal/readability beat such as root-hollow/log-run/cool-edge without adding cave-system breadth.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Strengthen an existing forest route clue or replay consequence tied to weather/process state.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.focus: Make the forest process-backed route support behavior explicit without changing route behavior: prove `Moist Edge` controller targeting and repair the stale `Moist Hollow` note-tabs chip expectation.
current_state: `forest-cool-edge` already becomes `Moist Edge` during the late wet `moisture-hold` process window
`forest-moisture-holders` already becomes `Moist Hollow` during the same process window
field-request tests already protect active process titles and stable filed identities
runtime smoke already covers forest replay notices and the live `Moist Hollow` shelf, but the note-tabs chip assertion is stale
main_acceptance: controller-level proof shows `hand-lens` can retarget active `Moist Edge` creek-bend inspection from a nearer non-fit candidate to a farther route-fit clue such as `salmonberry`

the `Moist Edge` proof stays ordinary notebook-fit behavior, not an active-clue alternate: `supportRetargetsInspect` true, `supportPrefersActiveClue` false, `Notebook fit: edge carrier`, and no `LENS CLUE` label
paired `note-tabs` coverage leaves the physical nearest inspectable alone while keeping the in-field chip progress-facing with `0/3 clues`
the existing `Moist Hollow` runtime smoke expectation is refreshed from the stale default chip to the current note-tabs progress chip

no route definitions, process-focus copy, filed-note text, lane-2 forest copy, station pages, support order, save schema, world-map focus priority, route-marker behavior, geometry, science copy, new UI, replay system, loadout, planner, or quest surface changes
baseline_verification: PASS npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts -t "Moisture Holders|Moist Edge|retarget|non-hand-lens"
FAIL npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Moist Hollow" on the stale note-tabs chip expectation

result: Added controller proof that `hand-lens` retargets active `Moist Edge` creek-bend inspection from a nearer non-fit `fir-cone` candidate to the farther `salmonberry` `edge-carrier` clue.
Confirmed the `Moist Edge` retarget is ordinary notebook-fit behavior: `supportRetargetsInspect` true, `supportPrefersActiveClue` false, `Notebook fit: edge carrier`, and no `LENS CLUE` label.
Added paired `note-tabs` coverage proving non-hand-lens support leaves the physical nearest inspectable alone while the in-field chip stays progress-facing with `0/3 clues`.
Refreshed the existing `Moist Hollow` runtime-smoke expectation from the stale default route-title chip to the current `0/3 clues` support-biased chip.
preserved: new UI, replay, loadout, planner, or quest surfaces
verification: PASS npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Moist Hollow|Moisture Holders"

confirmed: the `Moist Edge` controller proof stays behavior-neutral and test/report scoped
`hand-lens` retargets active `Moist Edge` creek-bend inspection from nearer non-fit `fir-cone` to farther `salmonberry` `edge-carrier`
the retarget is ordinary notebook-fit behavior, with `supportRetargetsInspect` true, `supportPrefersActiveClue` false, `Notebook fit: edge carrier`, and no `LENS CLUE` label
`note-tabs` leaves the physical nearest inspectable alone while keeping the progress-facing `0/3 clues` chip
the stale `Moist Hollow` runtime expectation now matches the current note-tabs support chip
verification: PASS npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Moist Hollow|Moisture Holders"

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

recommended_snapshot.reason: A debug-only loadable state after Hidden Hollow but before Moisture Holders is complete lets reviewers prove the forest station/map/request seam still points at Root Hollow after lane-2 copy refreshes.

expected_state: no Trail Stride, Route Marker, Coastal Scrub, High Pass, route filing, save-schema, or station-shell change
expected_assertions: guidedFieldSeason.stage forest-study with MOISTURE HOLDERS station note
world map current and focused locations remain forest
field station routes page keeps hand-lens / HAND LENS selected
route board remains COASTAL SHELTER LINE targeting forest with forest-study active and the existing Moisture Holders summary
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Moisture Holders|first field-season guidance"

lane_1_main_result.summary: Added the debug-only forest-moisture-holders save snapshot plus resolver, runtime, journal, map, station, and browser proof that the mid-forest seam still points at Root Hollow.

recommended_target.reason: This existing note already ties Root Hollow / filtered-return discoveries to roots, bark, damp shelter, and seep-floor layering, so it can carry forest tactile identity without adding breadth.

recommended_copy.summary: Root curtains catch drips and shade, keeping damp shelter above the seep floor.
recommended_copy.observationPrompt: Where do roots keep damp shelter above the seep?

preserve: existing forest route definitions and route controller behavior
forest spatial bands and traversal geometry

lane_2_main_result.summary: Refreshed the existing forest root-held shelter ecosystem-note copy around root curtains catching drips and shade above the seep floor, with focused resolver coverage.
verification: node -e copy budget check printed summary 79 / prompt 48
scope_preserved: no new species, routes, science-ledger rows, close-look entries, station/state/save behavior, route-controller behavior, or forest geometry
root-held-shelter metadata and unlock behavior preserved

lane_2_critic_result.summary: The root-held shelter copy refresh stays science-safe, budget-safe, and lane-2 scoped while preserving stable note metadata and resolver behavior.
